message:
🐛 fix: GitOps drift-check test stale api.post mock after #8044 (#8051)

PR #8044 (#7993 Phase 4) migrated GitOps.tsx from api.post to direct
fetch against kc-agent's /gitops/detect-drift, but GitOps.test.tsx was
still mocking api.post. The #6156 test ("renders drift check failure as
error state, not as synced") passed apiPostMock.mockRejectedValue but
the component never called api.post, so the route-agnostic fetch mock
returned ok+empty body and the component rendered "synced" — the exact
regression the test exists to prevent.

This is a TEST regression, not a CODE regression. GitOps.tsx still
correctly renders failed drift checks as an error state.

Replace apiPostMock with a route-aware fetch mock + per-test
driftFetchHandler that can simulate success, error, or drifted results.
Update the three tests that were referencing apiPostMock to use the new
handler.

Fixes #8049

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
